scenario: A team builds a custom REST Service Task that wraps the corporate API. Every team in the company uses it. The modeling group wants a single source of truth for its configuration: URL prefix, headers, retry policy. Different processes can override only the endpoint path.
question: Which feature provides this reusable configuration shell?

options.text: Custom Connector with hardcoded URL prefix requires Connector SDK code development, packaging, and deployment as a running Connector service. While a Custom Connector can enforce a URL prefix, it requires engineering effort beyond model-time configuration. Element Templates provide the same reusable configuration shell purely through JSON descriptor files without any runtime code. For standardising task configuration properties like URL prefix, headers, and retry policy, Element Templates are the simpler and more maintainable choice.

Shared BPMN library with reusable subprocesses addresses BPMN process structure reuse â€” extracting common flow patterns into separately-deployable subprocesses invokable via Call Activity. It does not address task configuration reuse. A subprocess library cannot enforce a URL prefix or retry policy on Service Tasks across different processes. Element Templates are the correct tool for sharing and enforcing task-level configuration properties across multiple BPMN files and processes in the organisation.

Process-level start variables provide runtime configuration values to a single process instance at startup. They are specific to one instance of one process, not shared across all processes in the organisation. A start variable holding a URL prefix would need to be passed to every process instance across all teams, requiring every process start to include the configuration. This is operationally fragile and provides no enforcement mechanism. Element Templates enforce configuration at model time across all processes.

Element Template published to organization scope is the canonical Camunda 8 mechanism for a reusable, standardised task configuration shell. Web Modeler allows Element Templates published at project, organization, or Marketplace scope. An org-scoped template provides default values and field-level override constraints for every modeler across all organisation projects. Modelers apply the template and can only change fields the author marked as overridable, enforcing consistent URL prefix, headers, and retry policy configuration. ###

a.text: Incorrect. Element Templates at org scope provide DRY task definitions with sensible defaults and field-level overrides. Web Modeler offers them in the template picker; modelers select and customise allowed fields only.

b.text: Incorrect. Custom Connector works but requires SDK code; Element Template is config-only.

c.text: Incorrect. Subprocess library handles BPMN reuse, not task-config reuse.

d.text: Correct. Start variables aren't reusable across processes.

explanation: Start variables aren't reusable across processes.
